template.android.description: Official STS2 Android template for wiring the Harmony patch, runtime package, and installation flow.
template.android.notes: Android launch is the active target, and the runtime bundle, patch DLL, and native libraries are all shipped from the template .textension.
android.steam.0004: Comparing the local directory with the latest Steam Windows depot build.
android.steam.0007: Grant storage access before downloading game files.
android.steam.0008: No version is selected, so game files cannot be downloaded.
android.steam.0009: The current version does not have a game directory yet.
android.steam.0010: Preparing repair download: fetching Steam depot metadata.
android.steam.0011: Reusing the last Steam verification result to build the download plan.
android.steam.0012: Verifying the current directory and collecting files that need repair.
android.steam.0013: Verification finished. Building the download plan.
android.steam.0014: The current directory is already up to date.
android.steam.0018: Game files are now synced with the latest Steam Windows build.
android.steam.0019: The download finished, but some files are still not aligned.
android.steam.0020: Grant storage access before running Steam verification.
android.steam.0021: No version is selected, so Steam verification cannot run.
android.steam.0022: The current version does not have a game directory yet.
android.steam.0023: Preparing Steam verification: fetching Steam depot metadata.
android.steam.0026: Steam verification did not return detailed file differences, so the repair plan cannot be built right now.
android.steam.0027: Reused the last Steam verification result: the current directory does not need downloads.
android.steam.0028: Reused the last Steam verification result to build the download plan. A verification will run again after download.
android.steam.0031: Local file scan completed. Starting the Steam verification task.
android.steam.0034: Steam verification did not return a result.
android.steam.0041: Failed to obtain the manifest request code. The current CM session may be invalid, or the depot / manifest / branch pairing may be incorrect.
android.steam.0044: Reading the manifest file list failed.
android.steam.0045: The manifest contains more files than the current list limit can handle, so precise verification cannot continue.
android.steam.0047: Failed to load the STS2 depot preflight result. Check network connectivity and whether the saved Steam session is still valid.
android.steam.0048: No usable Windows depot was found. The selected account may not have access to the required content.
android.steam.0049: The current directory matches the latest Steam Windows depot build; extra local files were ignored.
android.steam.0050: The current directory matches the latest Steam Windows depot build.
android.steam.0051: Missing or mismatched files were found. You can repair them with download. Extra local files do not count as a problem.
android.steam.0052: The download was paused and can be resumed later.
android.steam.0066: Failed to obtain the depot key: the selected Steam account may not own STS2 or the required Windows depot entitlement.
android.steam.0067: Failed to obtain the depot key: the account's CM session may be invalid. Please sign in to this Steam account again with the CM mode.
android.steam.0068: Failed to obtain the depot key: the current depot parameters are invalid, or Steam returned invalid depot metadata.
android.steam.0069: Failed to obtain the depot key. Make sure the selected Steam account owns the game and the CM session is still valid.
android.steam.0070: Steam returned access denied: the selected account may not own STS2 or the required Windows depot entitlement.
android.steam.0071: Steam returned an invalid session: the account's CM session may have expired. Please sign in again using the CM mode.
android.steam.0072: Steam returned invalid parameters: the current depot / manifest / branch pairing may be incorrect.
android.steam.0073: There seems to be a network problem, so Steam verification cannot finish right now.
android.steam.0074: The selected account is not a Steam account.
android.steam.0075: The selected account does not have a CM login that can be used for depot operations. Please sign in again with the CM mode.
android.steam.0076: Choose a saved Steam account first.
android.steam.0077: The selected account is not a Steam account.
android.steam.0078: The selected Steam account does not include the CM login type, so it cannot be used for STS2 Steam verification or download.
android.steam.0079: The selected Steam account has an invalid SteamID.
android.steam.0080: This launcher does not expose the Steam depot service required by the Android template.
android.entrypoint.0008: Game file verification and repair use the saved Steam account selected here.
android.entrypoint.0012: Game file verification and Steam Depot download are now shown separately, while the Steam branch is still shared for now.
android.entrypoint.0023: Recursively scan JSON manifests in the current version's mod directory and show each detected mod.
android.entrypoint.0025: This section shows the current version, mod directory, and the latest scan result.
android.entrypoint.0032: Select a current version on the home page first so the mod manager knows which directory to scan.
android.entrypoint.0034: The current version does not have a mod directory configured yet. Open the version detail to fill it in first.
android.entrypoint.0036: The configured mod directory for the current version does not exist yet. Check the path first.
android.entrypoint.0038: These JSON files were not recognized as valid mod manifests.
android.entrypoint.0039: This manifest file could not be parsed as a mod manifest.
android.entrypoint.0041: Each detected mod from the current version's mod directory is listed here.
android.entrypoint.0043: No valid mod manifest was detected in the current mod directory yet.
android.entrypoint.0044: This page shows the manifest, runtime files, and directory info for this mod.
android.entrypoint.0046: This section shows the manifest info and runtime file detection results for the current mod.
android.entrypoint.0054: Adjust the current version and performance settings here.
android.entrypoint.0056: Adjust the version and performance settings used by the home launch bar here.
android.entrypoint.0059: No version is selected yet, so launch cannot proceed.
android.entrypoint.0062: These settings are automatically written into the STS2Mobile launch context and take effect the next time the game starts.
android.entrypoint.0064: Select a current version first before adjusting STS2Mobile launch settings.
android.entrypoint.0067: Lists shader cache files managed by the Android template per release_info.json version.
android.entrypoint.0069: Launch uses only the cache matching the current game directory release_info.json version; missing caches trigger warmup first.
android.entrypoint.0071: Cache found for the current release.
android.entrypoint.0072: The current release has no cache yet; enabling shader cache will run warmup first.
android.entrypoint.0073: No valid version is selected, so the current release cannot be resolved.
android.entrypoint.0077: After enabling shader cache and completing warmup, generated cache files will appear here.
android.entrypoint.0081: Check and repair the Godot and .NET runtime files required for STS2 Android launch.
android.entrypoint.0083: Manage the target runtime directory assembled from the template package and the current version source directory.
android.entrypoint.0086: Only .dll files can be imported into the target runtime directory.
android.entrypoint.0091: Use Quick Fix or import DLLs to populate the target runtime directory.
android.entrypoint.0094: One STS2 instance can manage multiple versions and choose which one is current for launch.
android.entrypoint.0096: Tap an entry to select that version directly. Use the i button on the right to open the detail page. The selected version is highlighted.
android.entrypoint.0098: Create a new STS2 Android version for {0}.
android.entrypoint.0101: Create a version first before file checks, runtime fixes, or launch can proceed.
android.entrypoint.0103: Tap to select this version directly.
android.entrypoint.0106: Blank directories are filled with default Android paths based on the version number when saved.
android.entrypoint.0108: A new version is being created for {0}.
android.entrypoint.0111: This version is selected and can be launched or edited directly.
android.entrypoint.0112: Edit fields here or delete the version directly.
android.entrypoint.0118: Leave blank to use version number {0}; manual values only need to stay unique within the current instance.
android.entrypoint.0119: You can use any version number text, but it cannot duplicate another version in the same instance.
android.entrypoint.0123: Can be blank. The UI falls back to the version number.
android.entrypoint.0125: When creating a version, leaving this blank uses the default directory.
android.entrypoint.0128: When creating a version, leaving this blank uses the default directory.
android.entrypoint.0131: When creating a version, leaving this blank uses the default directory.
android.entrypoint.0133: Current release {0} has {1} cached files, {2} total.
android.entrypoint.0134: View and delete shader caches managed by the Android template; if the current release has no cache, warmup runs first.
android.entrypoint.0136: Leave blank to use 2. Must be an integer >= 1. Larger values reduce Spine update frequency.
android.entrypoint.0138: Lets STS2Mobile trim preloaded content more aggressively during launch, which usually saves memory.
android.entrypoint.0140: Leave blank to use 8. Must be an integer >= 0. Smaller values are usually steadier but load more slowly.
android.entrypoint.0142: Prefer the mobile shader path for the game; compatibility is usually better this way.
android.entrypoint.0144: Before the first launch, run a separate shader warmup pass; after it finishes, return to the launcher and reuse the cache on later launches.
android.entrypoint.0147: This directly decides whether Godot starts with Vulkan or OpenGL.
android.entrypoint.0149: Leave blank to use 50. Must be an integer between 0 and 100. Lower values reduce particle effects.
android.entrypoint.0151: Controls the intensity of glow-related visual effects. Unrecognized values automatically fall back to Reduced.
android.entrypoint.0153: Limit some extra VFX when the device needs an even lighter rendering load.
android.entrypoint.0159: Spine update divisor must be an integer >= 1.
android.entrypoint.0160: Asset loading batch size must be an integer >= 0.
android.entrypoint.0161: Particle scale percent must be an integer between 0 and 100.

android.entrypoint.0255: Disabled (the global mod switch in settings.save is off)
android.entrypoint.0259: settings.save could not be read, so this mod's enabled state cannot be changed right now.
android.entrypoint.0260: The current version does not have an available save directory yet, so the mod state cannot be written.
android.entrypoint.0261: The global mod switch in settings.save is currently off. Changing this will also turn the global mod switch back on.
android.entrypoint.0262: This toggle writes directly to the matching mod state in settings.save.
android.entrypoint.0263: This mod does not have an entry in settings.save yet, so it is currently treated as enabled by default. Toggling it writes an explicit state.

android.entrypoint.game_files.mode.locke: Verification mode is locked while checking or downloading.
android.entrypoint.game_files.mode.descr: Steam mode compares against the Windows depot; turning it off falls back to the simple key-file check.
android.entrypoint.game_files.branch.loc: Steam branch is locked while checking or downloading.
android.entrypoint.game_files.branch.des: Steam verification and repair download use the branch selected here.
android.steam.download.resuming_paused_t: Resuming the previously paused download task.
android.steam.download.preparing_repair_: Preparing the list of game files that need repair.
